biobtree_sonnet.response:
Based on my comprehensive analysis of DOORS syndrome using biobtree, here is the complete genetic profile:

## Complete Genetic Profile for DOORS Syndrome

### 1. Causally Linked Genes

**Two genes are causally linked to DOORS syndrome:**

- **TBC1D24** (TBC1 domain family member 24)
  - **Ensembl ID:** ENSG00000162065
  - **HGNC Symbol:** TBC1D24 (HGNC:29203)
  - **Evidence Level:** Definitive to Strong (multiple submitters)

- **ATP6V1B2** (ATPase H+ transporting V1 subunit B2)
  - **Ensembl ID:** ENSG00000147416
  - **HGNC Symbol:** ATP6V1B2 (HGNC:854)
  - **Evidence Level:** Supportive (Orphanet classification)

### 2. Pathogenic ClinVar Variants

**TBC1D24 gene:** At least **15+ pathogenic variants** identified, including:
- c.208G>T (p.Asp70Tyr) - rs587777147
- c.878G>C (p.Arg293Pro) - rs199700840
- c.115G>C (p.Ala39Pro) - rs770363653
- c.642_793del (p.Trp215fs) - rs2141872119
- c.715del (p.Val239fs) - rs2141872348

**ATP6V1B2 gene:** At least **6+ pathogenic variants** identified, including:
- c.1121A>G (p.Glu374Gly) - rs2128886555
- c.1454G>C (p.Arg485Pro) - rs730882177
- c.1516C>T (p.Arg506Ter) - rs794729667
- c.1192C>G (p.Leu398Val)
- c.1443G>A (p.Trp481Ter)
- Large deletion: NC_000008.10:g.(?_19362692)_(20112692_?)del

### 3. Database Identifiers

- **Orphanet ID:** 79500
- **OMIM Number:** 220500
- **MONDO ID:** MONDO:0009079
- **MeSH ID:** C563052

### 4. Associated Phenotypes (HPO Terms)

DOORS syndrome presents with a comprehensive phenotypic spectrum including:

**Core Features (Very Frequent 99-80%):**
- **HP:0000212** - Gingival overgrowth (Very frequent)

**Frequent Features (79-30%):**
- **HP:0000194** - Open mouth (Frequent)
- **HP:0000179** - Thick lower lip vermilion (Frequent)
- **HP:0000280** - Coarse facial features (Frequent)
- **HP:0000286** - Epicanthus (Frequent)
- **HP:0000294** - Low anterior hairline (Frequent)
- **HP:0000316** - Hypertelorism (Frequent)
- **HP:0000219** - Thin upper lip vermilion (Frequent)

**Occasional Features (29-5%):**
- **HP:0000079** - Urogenital abnormality (Occasional)
- **HP:0000121** - Nephrocalcinosis (Occasional)
- **HP:0000164** - Abnormality of the dentition (Occasional)
- **HP:0000175** - Cleft palate (Occasional)
- **HP:0000187** - Broad alveolar ridges (Occasional)
- **HP:0000189** - Narrow palate (Occasional)
- **HP:0000200** - Short lingual frenulum (Occasional)
- **HP:0000248** - Brachycephaly (Occasional)
- **HP:0000252** - Microcephaly (Occasional)
- **HP:0000269** - Prominent forehead (Occasional)

**Very Rare Features (<4-1%):**
- **HP:0000062** - Ambiguous genitalia (Very rare)
- **HP:0000126** - Hydronephrosis (Very rare)
- **HP:0000218** - High palate (Very rare)

### 5. Clinical Trials

**No specific clinical trials for DOORS syndrome were identified** in the current database. The clinical trials mentioned in the MONDO entry (NCT00397852, NCT00397995, etc.) appear to be related to other conditions that share some phenotypic overlap but are not specifically targeting DOORS syndrome.

### Summary

DOORS syndrome is an ultra-rare autosomal recessive disorder caused by mutations in **TBC1D24** (primary gene with definitive evidence) and **ATP6V1B2** (supportive evidence). The syndrome is characterized by **D**eafness, **O**nychodystrophy, **O**steodystrophy, intellectual disability (**R**etardation), and **S**eizures. With over 20 pathogenic variants identified across both genes and a comprehensive phenotypic spectrum documented through HPO terms, this represents a well-characterized genetic syndrome despite its rarity (~100 cases worldwide).

---
**Data Sources:**
- http://biobtree:9291/ws/?i=DOORS syndrome
- http://biobtree:9291/ws/entry/?i=MONDO:0009079&s=mondo
- http://biobtree:9291/ws/map/?i=TBC1D24,ATP6V1B2&m=>>ensembl>>clinvar[clinvar.germline_classification=="Pathogenic"]
- http://biobtree:9291/ws/map/?i=ATP6V1B2&m=>>ensembl>>clinvar[clinvar.germline_classification=="Pathogenic"]
- http://biobtree:9291/ws/map/?i=MONDO:0009079&m=>>orphanet>>hpo
- http://biobtree:9291/ws/entry/?i=79500&s=orphanet
- http://biobtree:9291/ws/map/?i=MONDO:0009079&m=>>clinical_trials
- http://biobtree:9291/ws/entry/?i=NCT00397852&s=clinical_trials
- http://biobtree:9291/ws/?i=TBC1D24,ATP6V1B2&s=ensembl
- http://biobtree:9291/ws/?i=ENSG00000162065,ENSG00000147416&s=ensembl
- http://biobtree:9291/ws/entry/?i=HP:0000212&s=hpo

biobtree_gpt41.response:
Here is a comprehensive genetic and clinical profile for **DOORS syndrome** (Deafness, Onychodystrophy, Osteodystrophy, Intellectual disability, and Seizures):

---

### 1. Causally Linked Genes (with Ensembl IDs and HGNC symbols)

**A. TBC1D24**
- **Ensembl ID:** [ENSG00000162065](https://www.ensembl.org/Homo_sapiens/Gene/Summary?g=ENSG00000162065)
- **HGNC Symbol:** TBC1D24 ([HGNC:29203](https://www.genenames.org/data/gene-symbol-report/#!/hgnc_id/HGNC:29203))

**B. ATP6V1B2**
- **Ensembl ID:** [ENSG00000147416](https://www.ensembl.org/Homo_sapiens/Gene/Summary?g=ENSG00000147416)
- **HGNC Symbol:** ATP6V1B2 ([HGNC:854](https://www.genenames.org/data/gene-symbol-report/#!/hgnc_id/HGNC:854))

---

### 2. Pathogenic ClinVar Variants

**TBC1D24 (ENSG00000162065):**
- At least **4 pathogenic ClinVar variants** directly linked to DOORS syndrome and related phenotypes:
  - [100677](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/clinvar/variation/100677) (p.Asp70Tyr)
  - [100678](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/clinvar/variation/100678) (p.Arg293Pro)
  - [1066340](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/clinvar/variation/1066340) (p.Ala39Pro)
  - [1071941](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/clinvar/variation/1071941) (p.Trp215fs)
- There are additional pathogenic variants in TBC1D24, but these are the most directly associated with DOORS syndrome.

**ATP6V1B2 (ENSG00000147416):**
- At least **5 pathogenic ClinVar variants**:
  - [1334193](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/clinvar/variation/1334193) (p.Glu374Gly)
  - [183414](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/clinvar/variation/183414) (p.Arg485Pro)
  - [203442](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/clinvar/variation/203442) (p.Arg506Ter)
  - [3245557](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/clinvar/variation/3245557) (large deletion)
  - [3362385](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/clinvar/variation/3362385) (p.Leu398Val)
  - [3377165](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/clinvar/variation/3377165) (p.Trp481Ter)

---

### 3. Orphanet ID and OMIM Number

- **Orphanet ID:** [ORPHA:79500](https://www.orpha.net/consor/cgi-bin/OC_Exp.php?Lng=GB&Expert=79500)
- **OMIM Number:** [220500](https://omim.org/entry/220500)

---

### 4. Associated Phenotypes (HPO Terms)

- The direct mapping to HPO terms failed, but based on clinical knowledge and the disease definition, the main HPO terms are:
  - **HP:0000365** – Sensorineural hearing impairment
  - **HP:0001773** – Onychodystrophy
  - **HP:0000939** – Osteodystrophy
  - **HP:0001249** – Intellectual disability
  - **HP:0001250** – Seizures
  - **HP:0001156** – Brachydactyly
  - **HP:0001263** – Developmental delay

  *Note: For a full, up-to-date HPO list, see the [Monarch Initiative page for DOORS syndrome](https://monarchinitiative.org/disease/mondo:0009079).*

---

### 5. Clinical Trials

- There are **no clinical trials specifically for DOORS syndrome**. The clinical trials mapped to the MONDO ID are for related symptoms (e.g., onychodystrophy, osteodystrophy, mental health), but not for DOORS syndrome itself.
- Example related trials:
  - [NCT02731222](https://clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT02731222): Onychodystrophy in chronic venous disease
  - [NCT05601271](https://clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT05601271): Bone metabolism markers
  - [NCT06541769](https://clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT06541769): Onychodystrophy in chemotherapy

---

**Summary Table**

| Gene      | Ensembl ID        | HGNC Symbol | Pathogenic ClinVar Variants |
|-----------|-------------------|-------------|----------------------------|
| TBC1D24   | ENSG00000162065   | TBC1D24     | ≥4                         |
| ATP6V1B2  | ENSG00000147416   | ATP6V1B2    | ≥5                         |

- **Orphanet:** ORPHA:79500
- **OMIM:** 220500
- **HPO:** Sensorineural hearing loss, onychodystrophy, osteodystrophy, intellectual disability, seizures, brachydactyly, developmental delay
- **Clinical trials:** None specific for DOORS syndrome

If you need more details on any variant or want a full HPO export, let me know!

---
**Data Sources:**

- http://biobtree:9291/ws/map/?i=MONDO:0009079&m=>>mondo>>orphanet

Of course. Here is a complete genetic profile of DOORS syndrome based on the latest data.

### 1. Causally Linked Genes

DOORS syndrome is causally linked to the following genes:

*   **TBC1D24**:
    *   Ensembl ID: `ENSG00000162065`
    *   HGNC Symbol: `TBC1D24` (HGNC:29203)
*   **ATP6V1B2**:
    *   Ensembl ID: `ENSG00000147416`
    *   HGNC Symbol: `ATP6V1B2` (HGNC:854)

### 2. Pathogenic ClinVar Variants

*   There are **4 pathogenic ClinVar variants** for the `TBC1D24` gene.
*   There are **no pathogenic ClinVar variants** listed for the `ATP6V1B2` gene in relation to DOORS syndrome.

### 3. Orphanet and OMIM IDs

*   **Orphanet ID**: `79500`
*   **OMIM Number**: `220500`

### 4. Associated Phenotypes (HPO Terms)

While a direct mapping to HPO terms was not available, the full name of DOORS syndrome itself describes its primary phenotypes: **D**eafness, **O**nychodystrophy, **O**steodystrophy, **R**etardation (impaired intellectual development), and **S**eizures.

### 5. Clinical Trials

There are currently **no clinical trials specifically for DOORS syndrome**. However, there are clinical trials for some of the individual symptoms associated with the syndrome, such as onychodystrophy and seizures.

---

web_chatgpt52.response:
Here’s a **comprehensive genetic and clinical profile** for *DOORS syndrome* (deafness, onychodystrophy, osteodystrophy, intellectual disability and seizures), incorporating authoritative identifiers, genes, variants, phenotypes (HPO), and clinical trial data:

---

## 🧬 1. **Genes Causally Linked to DOORS Syndrome**

### **TBC1D24**

* **HGNC symbol:** **TBC1D24** (HGNC:29203)([genecards.org][1])
* **Ensembl Gene ID:** **ENSG00000162065**([ensembl.org][2])
* **Inheritance:** Autosomal **recessive** for classical DOORS syndrome with biallelic pathogenic variants identified in ~50% of families studied.([NCBI][3])
* **OMIM gene entry:** *613577* (via GeneCards linking)([genecards.org][1])

### **ATP6V1B2**

* **HGNC symbol:** **ATP6V1B2** (HGNC:854)([search.thegencc.org][4])
* **Ensembl gene ID:** (Standard human gene Ensembl ID not readily available in searches; gene is well characterized in HGNC/GenCC and GeneCards)([search.thegencc.org][4])
* **Inheritance:** Predominantly **autosomal dominant** for **DDOD** (deafness-onychodystrophy), but truncating variants (e.g., p.Arg506*) have been reported in individuals clinically diagnosed with **DOORS syndrome** or overlapping features, expanding its causal spectrum.([ResearchGate][5])

> **Note:**   TBC1D24 is the *primary* gene causally linked with classical DOORS syndrome. ATP6V1B2 is *emerging* as a second gene with overlap and reported cases but with variable expressivity.

---

## 📍 2. **Pathogenic ClinVar Variants by Gene**

> **Methodological note:** Official “counts” in ClinVar vary by classification and submissions; below is based on documented entries *linked to disease phenotypes*.

### **TBC1D24 – ClinVar (selected pathogenic variants)**

* Example pathogenic entries linked with DOORS and related phenotypes include:

  * **NM_001199107.2(TBC1D24):c.724C>T (p.Arg242Cys)** – Pathogenic for DOORS in ClinVar.([NCBI][6])
  * **NM_001199107.2(TBC1D24):c.1008del (p.His336fs)** – Pathogenic for DOORS syndrome.([NCBI][7])
  * Additional pathogenic/likely pathogenic missense variants such as **p.Ala500Val** have been classified in ClinVar and literature in compound heterozygous states.([NCBI][8])

→ **Approximate pathogenic records for TBC1D24 in ClinVar:** *many pathogenic/likely pathogenic variants across epilepsy- and hearing-related disorders (including DOORS phenotypes).* A precise total requires a Wilbur query but totals likely >10 distinct pathogenic allele records.([clinvarminer.genetics.utah.edu][9])

### **ATP6V1B2 – ClinVar**

* Specific pathogenic variant **p.Arg506*** (NM_001693.4:c.1516C>T) is associated with DDOD and reported in DOORS syndrome phenotypes. While not always separately submitted under DOORS syndrome, this allele is recognized pathogenic.([ResearchGate][5])

→ **ATP6V1B2 pathogenic variants in ClinVar** are fewer but include this recurrent truncating allele.

---

## 🧬 3. **Orphanet & OMIM Numbers**

* **Orphanet ID:** **ORPHA:79500** – *DOORS syndrome*([orpha.net][10])
* **OMIM number:** **220500** (DOOR/DOORS syndrome)([NCBI][3])

---

## 🧠 4. **Associated Phenotypes (HPO Terms)**

Characteristic clinical features linked with DOORS include:

| HPO Term   | Description                                             |
| ---------- | ------------------------------------------------------- |
| HP:0000365 | **Sensorineural hearing impairment** (deafness)         |
| HP:0001802 | **Abnormal nail morphology** (onychodystrophy)          |
| HP:0002653 | **Abnormality of the skeletal system** (osteodystrophy) |
| HP:0001249 | **Intellectual disability**                             |
| HP:0002069 | **Seizures**                                            |
| HP:0012696 | **Triphalangeal thumb**                                 |
| HP:0002663 | **Short distal phalanx of finger**                      |
| HP:0001183 | **Polyneuropathy**                                      |
| HP:0002068 | **Cerebral atrophy**                                    |
| HP:0000085 | **Abnormality of the kidney (e.g., renal agenesis)**    |

*(Terms drawn from aggregated clinical descriptions in MedGen/Orphanet; exact HPO codes can vary by database export.)*([NCBI][3])

---

## 🧪 5. **Clinical Trials for DOORS Syndrome**

**Direct trials labeled specifically for “DOORS syndrome”** are *not registered* on ClinicalTrials.gov. Searches with the term yield general rare disease registries but no interventional or gene-specific DOORS-targeted trials:

* *NCT01793168* — Rare Disease Patient Registry & Natural History (general registry for rare diseases). No DOORS-specific intervention arm listed.([ClinicalTrials.gov][11])

Other genetic disease/tracking registries may include participants with DOORS features, but **no active clinical trials are focused solely on DOORS syndrome** as of available registry data.

---

## 📌 Summary

| Item                            | Key Findings                                                                      |
| ------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Primary causal gene**         | *TBC1D24* (recessive)                                                             |
| **Additional gene implicated**  | *ATP6V1B2* (variants with overlap)                                                |
| **Ensembl IDs**                 | *TBC1D24*: ENSG00000162065                                                        |
| **HGNC symbols**                | *TBC1D24*, *ATP6V1B2*                                                             |
| **ClinVar pathogenic variants** | Multiple for TBC1D24; key truncating variant p.Arg506* for ATP6V1B2               |
| **Orphanet ID**                 | ORPHA:79500                                                                       |
| **OMIM number**                 | 220500                                                                            |
| **HPO phenotypes**              | Deafness, nail anomalies, bone anomalies, intellectual disability, seizures, etc. |
| **Clinical trials**             | None disease-specific; only general rare disease registries                       |

---

If you’d like, I can also provide an *annotated list of specific pathogenic TBC1D24 variants* with coordinates and clinical features, or a downloadable gene-phenotype spreadsheet.

web_gemini3_pro.response:
### 1. Causally Linked Genes

DOORS syndrome is genetically heterogeneous, but the primary cause is biallelic mutations in *TBC1D24*. More recently, specific variants in *ATP6V1B2* (often associated with the overlapping Dominant Deafness-Onychodystrophy syndrome, DDOD) have been identified in patients meeting the DOORS clinical criteria.

| Gene Symbol | HGNC ID | Ensembl ID | Inheritance Pattern | Role |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **TBC1D24** | **[HGNC:29203](https://www.genenames.org/data/gene-symbol-report/#!/hgnc_id/HGNC:29203)** | **ENSG00000162065** | Autosomal Recessive | Primary cause. Encodes a TBC1 domain family member involved in vesicle trafficking and oxidative stress response. |
| **ATP6V1B2** | **[HGNC:854](https://www.google.com/search?q=https://www.genenames.org/data/gene-symbol-report/%23!/hgnc_id/HGNC:854)** | **ENSG00000116133** | Autosomal Dominant | Secondary cause (often *de novo*). Encodes a subunit of the vacuolar H+-ATPase. Associated with DDOD and Zimmerman-Laband syndromes, which phenotypically overlap with DOORS. |

---

### 2. Pathogenic ClinVar Variants

*Counts are approximate and fluctuate as new submissions are reviewed.*

* **TBC1D24:** **>60 Pathogenic/Likely Pathogenic variants**
* *Context:* There are over 1,000 total reported variants in ClinVar for this gene. The pathogenic variants are diverse, including missense, frameshift, and splice-site mutations.
* *Note:* *TBC1D24* is also associated with other conditions like familial infantile myoclonic epilepsy (FIME) and nonsyndromic hearing loss, so not all pathogenic variants in this gene strictly result in the full DOORS phenotype.


* **ATP6V1B2:** **<15 Pathogenic/Likely Pathogenic variants**
* *Context:* Total variants reported are fewer (~170).
* *Specific Link:* The nonsense variant **c.1516C>T (p.Arg506*)** is recurrently found in patients presenting with the DOORS phenotype.



---

### 3. Registry Identifiers

| Database | ID / Number | Link |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Orphanet** | **ORPHA:79500** | [Orphanet Entry](https://www.orpha.net/en/disease/detail/79500) |
| **OMIM** | **#220500** | [OMIM Entry](https://www.omim.org/entry/220500) |

---

### 4. Phenotypic Profile (HPO Terms)

The syndrome is defined by its acronym. The following Human Phenotype Ontology (HPO) terms correspond to these cardinal features and other common findings.

#### **D** - Deafness

* **HP:0000407** | **Sensorineural hearing loss** (Profound, congenital)

#### **O** - Onychodystrophy

* **HP:0001805** | **Onychodystrophy** (Malformation of nails)
* **HP:0001799** | **Anonychia** (Absence of nails, often on fingers/toes)
* **HP:0001800** | **Hypoplastic fingernails**

#### **O** - Osteodystrophy

* **HP:0009882** | **Short distal phalanx of finger** (Brachydactyly)
* **HP:0001199** | **Triphalangeal thumb** (Thumb has three phalanges instead of two)

#### **R** - Intellectual Disability (formerly Mental Retardation)

* **HP:0001249** | **Intellectual disability** (Ranging from moderate to profound)
* **HP:0001263** | **Global developmental delay**

#### **S** - Seizures

* **HP:0001250** | **Seizures** (Often generalized tonic-clonic or myoclonic)
* **HP:0002123** | **Generalized myoclonic seizure**

#### **Additional Features**

* **HP:0003125** | **Increased urinary alpha-ketoglutarate** (Elevated 2-oxoglutarate is a metabolic marker)
* **HP:0000465** | **Webbed neck**
* **HP:0000414** | **Bulbous nose** (Coarse facial features)

---

### 5. Clinical Trials

* **Status:** There are currently **no active interventional clinical trials** (drug therapies) specifically recruiting for DOORS syndrome.
* **Research Focus:** Most current research is limited to **Natural History Studies** and basic science focusing on the *TBC1D24* pathway (oxidative stress and vesicle transport) to identify potential future drug targets.
* **Registry:** Patients may enroll in general rare disease registries (e.g., Coffin-Siris syndrome registries often include overlapping phenotypes) to facilitate future trials.

Would you like me to find the specific contact information for a rare disease center of excellence that specializes in *TBC1D24*-related disorders?

web_claude46.response:
Here's a summary of the key findings:

**Causal genes:** Two genes are causally linked — **TBC1D24** (HGNC:29203, Ensembl: ENSG00000162065) on chromosome 16p13.3 (autosomal recessive, biallelic), and **ATP6V1B2** (HGNC:854, Ensembl: ENSG00000147416) on chromosome 8p21.3 (autosomal dominant, typically de novo). TBC1D24 is the most frequently identified cause, while ATP6V1B2 with the recurrent p.Arg506* truncating variant was identified in TBC1D24-negative families.

**Disease identifiers:** Orphanet ORPHA:79500, OMIM #220500, MONDO:0009079.

**ClinVar variants:** TBC1D24 has approximately 50–60 pathogenic and 30–40 likely pathogenic variants across all associated phenotypes; ATP6V1B2 has a smaller pool (~5–10 P/LP). I recommend querying ClinVar directly for real-time counts, as the database is updated continuously.

**Clinical trials:** Approximately 50% of affected individuals have no identified TBC1D24 mutation, and no disease-specific clinical trials are currently registered, consistent with the ultra-rare status of the disorder.

# DOORS Syndrome — Comprehensive Genetic Profile

**DOORS** = **D**eafness, **O**nychodystrophy, **O**steodystrophy, intellectual disability (formerly mental **R**etardation), **S**eizures

---

## 1. Causally Linked Genes

### Primary gene: *TBC1D24*

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **HGNC Symbol** | TBC1D24 |
| **HGNC ID** | HGNC:29203 |
| **Ensembl ID** | ENSG00000162065 |
| **OMIM (gene)** | 613577 |
| **Locus** | 16p13.3 |
| **UniProtKB** | Q9ULP9 |
| **Protein** | TBC1 domain family member 24 (Rab-GTPase–activating protein with TLDc domain) |
| **Inheritance** | Autosomal recessive (biallelic pathogenic variants) |
| **Mechanism** | Loss of function — impairs vesicular trafficking and oxidative-stress resistance in neurons and inner-ear hair cells |

TBC1D24 is the most frequently identified causal gene, accounting for roughly 50% of genetically confirmed DOORS cases (9 of 18 families with all five cardinal features in the landmark Campeau et al. 2014 exome study).

### Secondary gene: *ATP6V1B2*

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **HGNC Symbol** | ATP6V1B2 |
| **HGNC ID** | HGNC:854 |
| **Ensembl ID** | ENSG00000147416 |
| **OMIM (gene)** | 606939 |
| **Locus** | 8p21.3 |
| **UniProtKB** | P21281 |
| **Protein** | ATPase H⁺-transporting V1 subunit B2 (vacuolar proton pump, brain isoform) |
| **Inheritance** | Autosomal dominant (typically de novo) |
| **Key variant** | c.1516C>T; p.(Arg506*) — a recurrent truncating variant found in 9 individuals from 8 unrelated families with DOORS syndrome (Beauregard-Lacroix et al., *Genet Med* 2021) |

ATP6V1B2 was identified in TBC1D24-negative DOORS families and is also associated with dominant deafness-onychodystrophy (DDOD) syndrome. The same p.Arg506* variant can produce either DDOD or full DOORS phenotype, suggesting these are part of a clinical spectrum.

> **Note on genetic heterogeneity:** Approximately half of clinically diagnosed DOORS patients lack mutations in either gene, indicating additional loci remain to be discovered. A related condition, OORS syndrome (onychodystrophy, osteodystrophy, intellectual disability, seizures — without deafness; OMIM 619356), is caused by biallelic variants in *PIGF*.

---

## 2. Pathogenic / Likely Pathogenic ClinVar Variants

Exact counts fluctuate as ClinVar is updated continuously. The approximate figures below are based on the most recent available data:

| Gene | Pathogenic (P) | Likely Pathogenic (LP) | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **TBC1D24** | ~50–60 | ~30–40 | Literature documents over 50 distinct pathogenic mutations across the TBC and TLDc domains (missense, nonsense, frameshift, splice-site). A 2020 study noted ~96 total variants in ClinVar/LOVD databases across all clinical significance categories. These variants span all TBC1D24-related phenotypes (DOORS, DEE, FIME, DFNB86, DFNA65), not DOORS alone. |
| **ATP6V1B2** | ~5–10 | ~3–5 | A smaller variant pool. The recurrent p.Arg506* is the variant most consistently linked to DOORS. Other variants (p.Glu374Gln, p.Arg485Pro, p.Leu398Val) are associated with DDOD or Zimmermann-Laband syndrome. |

> **To obtain real-time counts**, query ClinVar directly:
> - `TBC1D24[gene] AND ("pathogenic"[clinical_significance] OR "likely pathogenic"[clinical_significance])`
> - `ATP6V1B2[gene] AND ("pathogenic"[clinical_significance] OR "likely pathogenic"[clinical_significance])`

## 5. Clinical Trials

**No clinical trials specifically for DOORS syndrome are currently registered** on ClinicalTrials.gov or the EU Clinical Trials Register, consistent with the ultra-rare nature of the disorder (~100 cases worldwide).

However, several related avenues exist:

- **Natural history / registry studies**: The TBC1D24 Foundation actively connects families and facilitates research participation. The Orphanet registry lists research projects related to ATP6V1B2 and TBC1D24.
- **Symptomatic management trials**: Patients may be eligible for broader epilepsy trials (e.g., trials of anti-seizure medications for drug-resistant genetic epilepsies or developmental and epileptic encephalopathies).
- **Cochlear implantation**: Case reports document cochlear implant outcomes in DOORS patients with profound deafness, though no formal trial exists.

> **Recommended resources for trial monitoring:**
> - [ClinicalTrials.gov](https://clinicaltrials.gov) — search "DOORS syndrome" or "TBC1D24"
> - [EU Clinical Trials Register](https://www.clinicaltrialsregister.eu)
> - [Orphanet ORPHA:79500](https://www.orpha.net/en/disease/detail/79500) — lists research projects and registries

review_1.key_finding: For ultra-rare DOORS syndrome (~100 cases worldwide), Gemini hallucinated Ensembl ID ENSG00000116133 for ATP6V1B2 (correct: ENSG00000147416). All LLMs estimated ~15 ClinVar variants for ATP6V1B2 while biobtree returned 192 - a 13x undercount.
review_1.biobtree_advantage: Returns verified 192 ClinVar variants for ATP6V1B2 and 178 HPO phenotypes. Complete enumeration impossible for LLMs even with web search.

disease_id_accuracy.all_llms: Correct Orphanet (79500) and OMIM (220500) - even ultra-rare disease IDs findable via web

review_1.quotable: For the ultra-rare DOORS syndrome, Gemini hallucinated Ensembl ID ENSG00000116133 for ATP6V1B2 (correct: ENSG00000147416) — a database identifier error that would corrupt downstream analysis. All LLMs estimated ~15 ClinVar variants while biobtree returned 192.
review_1.notes: Despite only ~100 cases worldwide, LLMs correctly found Orphanet/OMIM IDs via web search. However, enumeration limitations persist: 13x undercount for ClinVar, 89-95% gap for HPO phenotypes. Ensembl ID hallucination risk demonstrated.
